violations.description: All tests use fixed thread IDs, increasing risk of cross-test state leakage when run concurrently against mutable environments.
violations.suggestion: Suffix thread IDs per test worker or inject per-test namespace in fixture factory.

violations.description: Lifecycle-mutating outbound actions run against shared story thread identities without explicit teardown/reset hooks.
violations.suggestion: Add test-scoped thread seeds or cleanup reset endpoint after mutation tests.

violations.description: Each E2E test reuses same login path and actor identity without explicit isolation assertions.
violations.suggestion: Introduce test-scoped auth/session fixture with clearer setup boundary.

recommendations: Make thread IDs test-scoped to avoid order-dependent behavior
Use isolated auth/session fixture boundaries for E2E cases
summary: Isolation is good but shared story identities create medium risk in parallel or stateful environments.
